test_result.test: compare word and glyph entropies between the herbal and other sections using a two-sample kolmogorov
test_result.details: Section entropy std dev: 0.0593. Range: 3.7149 - 3.9016. Variable across sections.

scored_hypotheses.claim: The text uses a simple substitution cipher on medieval Latin, which would produce glyph entropy ~4.0

test_result.details: The observed glyph entropy of 3.8627 bits is lower than the expected ~4.0 bits for a simple substitution cipher on medieval Latin, contradicting the hypothesis.

test_result.test: compare word length distributions between the herbal and other sections using a two-sample k-s test
test_result.details: Observed avg word length: 5.03. Expected: ~5.0. Difference: 0.03.

scored_hypotheses.claim: The Currier A/B split reflects a genuine linguistic difference between two distinct languages or dia

test_result.test: compare word and glyph distributions between currier a and b using a chi-squared test
test_result.details: Language A: entropy=3.8321, 3513 unique/11022 total words. Language B: entropy=3.8611, 5089 unique/23766 total words. Entropy difference: 0.0290. Vocabulary density difference: 0.1046.

scored_hypotheses.claim: The text is encoded using a simple substitution cipher on a language other than Latin or Italian.

test_result.details: The hypothesis is contradicted by the high glyph entropy (3.8627 bits), which suggests a complex encoding scheme, and the Zipf exponent (0.8946) is close to 1, indicating a language with a simple word frequency distribution, both of which are more consistent with Latin or Italian languages than a simple substitution cipher on an unknown language.
